title: Show-Off Creamy Venezuelan Dried Chilli Empanadas
description: A showy, creamy twist on traditional Venezuelan empanadas featuring dried chillies, ideal for a dinner party or special occasion.
instructions: Combine 1 cup warm water and 1 teaspoon dried yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes until yeast is activated.
Mix 2 cups all-purpose flour, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 cup vegetable oil in a bowl.
Add the yeast mixture to the dry ingredients and knead for 5 minutes until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest in a warm place for 1 hour.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add 4 Venezuelan dried aji amarillo chillies and cook for 5 minutes until they're slightly puffed and fragrant.
Mash the cooked chillies in a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder until they're well-crushed.
In a large bowl, combine 1 cup creme fraiche, 1/2 cup shredded Manchego cheese, 2 tablespoons white wine vinegar, and crushed chillies. Mix well until combined.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch and use a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the chilli-cream mixture in the center of each dough circle and top with a sprinkle of shredded cheese.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Use a fork to crimp the edges of the empanada and brush with a little water.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20-25 minutes until golden brown.
Let the empanadas cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with chopped cilantro, scallions, and a sprinkle of grated cheese.
